meaning: n.冲突,打架,争论,(织物等)磨损处vt.使磨损vi.被磨损
examples: The stitching had begun to fray at the edges.
They were ready for the fray.
As the debate went on, tempers began to fray.
In the past couple of years the big benefits management firms have joined the fray.
Mr Byers return to the political fray came as Mr Blair backed his beleaguered transport secretary.
